claim_boundary: HOODIE remains the Feature 080 proposed method boundary.
No thesis method, DCQ method, or custom queue redesign is introduced.
The audit documents interface-only DRL/LSTM/forecast boundaries where full training is not reproduced.
No full empirical HOODIE reproduction claim is made.
invalid_label_check: Legacy active-label check passed: retired aliases are absent from the active 086 outputs.
Historical references remain in prior feature documentation only.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Task source / IoT layer, edge agents, and cloud are represented as a three-tier CEC topology.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the deterministic topology abstraction; no new simulator topology is claimed.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime layer uses deterministic topology profiles and scenario fixtures to represent the source, edge, and cloud tiers.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Multiple edge agents plus one cloud node exist as active runtime participants.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Treat the active policy set and scenario contexts as the evidence boundary, not as a full distributed runtime simulator.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime policy set and scenario contexts model multiple edge agents plus a cloud destination.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Horizontal EA-to-EA destinations must be legal only when topology permits them.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The legality model is documented through candidate/action legality checks.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The policy layer distinguishes legal horizontal destinations from illegal attempts and rejects invalid horizontal choices.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Vertical offload reaches the cloud path and uses cloud-side execution capacity.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The cloud path is evidenced by scenario traces, not by a separate cloud simulator service.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime scenarios include cloud-vertical fallback and vertical offload decisions with cloud destinations.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Tasks carry ID, data size, CPU demand/processing density, and deadline/timeout semantics.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The task model is reconstructed through deterministic scenario fixtures and runtime rows.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: TaskBlueprint and scenario rows retain task_id, workload, deadline pressure, and arrival/completion timing fields.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Workload arrivals are represented over time or documented as deterministic approximations.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the deterministic workload approximation; do not claim stochastic arrival fidelity.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Scenario definitions encode workload and deadline-pressure profiles deterministically with seeds.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Local/private queue behavior contributes waiting and completion time before local execution finishes.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: This is an evidence-layer queue approximation, not a claim that the full FIFO queue simulator exists.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Queue-length observations and delay traces are captured in the runtime rows, but the queue is represented through deterministic evidence rather than a standalone queue engine.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Offloaded tasks use offloading queues and transmission-time evidence before destination execution.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The evidence is candidate-based, not a full queue-simulation replay.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Horizontal and vertical candidate traces expose delay components and queue-length snapshots for offloaded actions.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Public/cloud queues receive vertically offloaded tasks and expose cloud-side waiting/execution behavior.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The cloud queue is an audit abstraction and not a dedicated runtime queue class.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The cloud path appears in the runtime outcomes and scenario traces; public/cloud queue behavior is documented through output timing rather than a separate queue model.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Local execution delay contributes to completion time.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Local delay is evidenced in runtime rows, not by a standalone physical simulator.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Local actions surface delay values and completion times in raw rows and aggregate metrics.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Horizontal offload delay includes transmission delay between edge agents.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No additional repair required for the evidence layer.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: MLEO candidate traces expose horizontal transmission_delay and total_delay components.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Vertical offload delay includes EA-to-cloud transmission delay.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No additional repair required for the evidence layer.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Vertical candidates in the MLEO and policy traces expose transmission_delay and total_delay components.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Remote/cloud execution delay contributes to total delay for vertical offload.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The remote compute term is evidence-backed, not a separate cloud runtime service.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Cloud-bearing candidates carry compute_delay and total_delay evidence in the MLEO trace and runtime rows.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Waiting time and completion time are explicit parts of task latency accounting.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Completion timing is represented in the audit layer, not as a full event simulator.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Runtime rows capture arrival_time, completion_time, delay, and queue_length_observations.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Timeout, drop, and unavailability are reflected in output outcomes.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The audit tracks these outcomes explicitly; it does not claim a full event-level simulator.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Runtime rows and aggregate metrics include dropped_timeout, dropped_unavailable, and illegal_action_rejected flags.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Local, horizontal, and vertical actions exist as a hybrid offloading action space.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No repair required; the hybrid action contract is already active.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Active policies expose local, horizontal, and vertical families and the runtime policy set is the paper set.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Local-vs-offload and destination selection are distinct or are clearly documented when collapsed.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the single-stage runtime approximation instead of pretending the training-stage boundary is reproduced.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime policy adapters operate as a documented single-stage approximation over legal candidate actions.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: HOODIE remains the proposed method boundary and is not renamed or expanded into a thesis/DCQ method.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No thesis/DCQ/custom-method claim is allowed.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: Feature 085/086 artifacts keep HOODIE as the proposed method boundary and do not introduce an alternate method claim.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Only RO, FLC, VO, HO, BCO, and MLEO are active baselines.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No legacy active labels remain in the 086 outputs.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime evaluation policy registry exposes exactly the paper baselines plus HOODIE.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: MLEO selects the legal candidate with minimum estimated total latency, not queue length only.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No repair required for the evidence layer; keep the numeric proof.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The candidate trace test proves MLEO chooses the minimum total_delay candidate when queue-length minimum is different.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Reward/cost equations remain inside the paper’s comparison boundary and are not overclaimed.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Do not claim a trained end-to-end reward model; keep the boundary conservative.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The runtime audit reports reward, delay, and drop outcomes with explicit claim-boundary text.

mechanism_coverage.paper_expectation: Metrics are classified so that paper-headline comparison and repository diagnostics are not conflated.
mechanism_coverage.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Do not promote repository diagnostics to paper headline metrics.
mechanism_coverage.simulator_behavior: The metric readiness matrix classifies primary, secondary, and diagnostic metrics explicitly.

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: paper OCR defines average delay / completion-delay comparison and runtime rows expose completion time and delay.
paper OCR compares drop ratio and runtime rows expose timeout/drop/unavailability outcomes.
Derived from completed and dropped rows in the runtime evaluation bundle.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: derived reliability metric for supporting analysis

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Useful for simulator validation; do not present as a paper headline unless separately justified.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: runtime diagnostic; not a paper headline metric

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Tracks availability failures as a simulator diagnostic.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: runtime diagnostic; not a paper headline metric

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Tracks deadline misses as a validation aid, not as a headline paper metric.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: runtime diagnostic; not a paper headline metric

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Reward output exists in runtime rows but the audit does not claim a full training-loop reproduction.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: supporting optimization metric with an explicit claim boundary

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Aggregate reward is exposed for analysis and marked as a supporting metric only.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: supporting optimization metric with an explicit claim boundary

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Throughput is retained as a secondary comparison metric, not a headline one.
metric_readiness_matrix.paper_use: supporting system-throughput metric with an explicit claim boundary

metric_readiness_matrix.evidence: Queue-stability score is an audit helper, not a paper metric.
Counts illegal-action rejections to validate legality handling.

scenario_mechanism_coverage.evidence: Runtime rows include low-load local and offload decisions with completed-delay evidence.
This is the divergent HOODIE/MLEO scenario with explicit delay-vs-drop behavior.
Horizontal offload is legal and exercised in runtime rows and policy tests.
Illegal horizontal destination attempts are rejected in policy traces.
Cloud fallback exercises the vertical/cloud path and cloud delay components.
Timeout/drop behavior is exercised and the HOODIE/MLEO divergence is documented here.
Mixed candidate sets exercise local, horizontal, and vertical candidate selection.

scope_proof: Active policies are the paper set: HOODIE, RO, FLC, VO, HO, BCO, MLEO.
No legacy active labels are exposed in the Feature 086 outputs.
MLEO evidence remains a policy-evidence boundary, not a new proposed method.
